# Autopilot for Codex

Autopilot for Codex is a native macOS menu bar app for monitoring and controlling local Codex automations.

## Summary

The app reads local Codex automation files from `~/.codex/automations`, shows automation status in the macOS menu bar, and gives developers a quiet control surface for review, approval, and operational visibility.

## Primary Audience

Developers and power users running OpenAI Codex automations on macOS.

## Key Features

- macOS menu bar status for local Codex automations.
- Compact control window for automation review.
- Approval-focused view with proposed changes and permissions.
- Local-first data handling.
- Notifications, launch at login, background stop/resume, and keyboard shortcuts.
- Signed, notarized Developer ID DMG release.

## Install

Download the latest DMG from:
https://github.com/dot-RealityTest/autopilot-for-codex/releases/latest

## Repository

https://github.com/dot-RealityTest/autopilot-for-codex

## Documentation

- README: https://github.com/dot-RealityTest/autopilot-for-codex#readme
- Overview: docs/overview.md
- Distribution: DISTRIBUTION.md

## Keywords

macOS menu bar app, Codex automation, OpenAI Codex, local-first developer tool, SwiftUI app, AppKit status bar utility, automation approval flow, autonomous workflow control.
